{"id":"broadside-r9da.61","title":"delivery.rs: circuit breaker counts 410 as failure before delete","description":"LOCATION: src/delivery.rs:303-338, process_batch() 410 arm

FINDING: When a delivery returns HTTP 410 Gone, the code removes the delivery queue entry and deletes matching followers, but does NOT call breaker.record_success(). It also does NOT call breaker.record_failure(). The result is neutral — the breaker is not updated. However, the 'processed += 1' at line 351 runs after the match block, so the 410 arm correctly counts the item. This is not a bug in the circuit breaker.

The real bug in the 410 arm: the DELETE of followers matches on 'inbox_uri = ? OR shared_inbox_uri = ?' (line 319). After a 410, the follower record should be deleted, but if the 410 came from a shared inbox (i.e., the follower's individual inbox_uri is different from the shared_inbox_uri that was used for delivery), the DELETE condition 'inbox_uri = inbox_uri_of_delivery OR shared_inbox_uri = inbox_uri_of_delivery' will not match the follower whose shared_inbox_uri differs from what we stored. It will match all followers who happened to share that inbox URI, even from other personas not related to this delivery.

IMPACT: A 410 Gone from a shared inbox can remove followers for ALL personas served by that shared inbox, not just the persona whose post triggered the delivery.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mark@reviewcommit.com","created_at":"2026-07-20T01:56:00.783002829Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-20T01:56:00.783002829Z","labels":["correctness","review"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"broadside-r9da.61","depends_on_id":"broadside-r9da","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-20T01:56:00.815180638Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value: {"title":"delivery.rs: 410 Gone deletes followers across all personas when post not found"}
Fixed: 410 with missing post skips follower delete instead of unscoped delete
